entries.what_they_read: Caught 'Trade player tokens that move with real stats' up top, then the $100 claim button and the 'no real money, no seed phrase' line underneath it.
entries.their_interpretation: It's like a fantasy sports sim but with token price action — fake money, real NBA stats driving the prices, and you're basically trying to front-run other people's reads on players.
entries.their_rationale: No seed phrase, no signup, free fake hundo — zero downside to just getting in and seeing what the boards look like. If the vibes are off inside I'll just close the tab.
confusion_notes: 'fair-value price' vs 'market price' split — I get it conceptually but I want to see the actual numbers before I trust the gap is real
'Buying raises the price for the next person' — is this a bonding curve thing? Feels like it but they never say bonding curve
'AWAITING TIP-OFF' at the top — wasn't sure if the app was broken or that's just flavor text for tonight's games
first_impression_elements: 'no real money · no seed phrase' subtext under the button — that's what made it feel safe enough to proceed
'AWAITING TIP-OFF' banner — caught my eye first, thought the site might be loading
ignored_elements: '↓Terms explained' link — didn't even register it
The 'Thesis' section with the 'being right early' copy — skimmed past it

checkpoint.q1_what_am_i_buying: Like a fake stock for a player, bro. LeBron token, whatever. It's play money so not real but the price moves like it's real.
checkpoint.q2_what_moves_price: Stats do something to it, and then other people buying or selling moves it too. If I buy it goes up for the next guy, so if everyone's aping in it pumps.
checkpoint.q3_hold_week: Honestly no idea, I'd never hold a week lmao. I guess the stats keep updating so the fair value thing changes? Might pump might dump. I'm not holding anything that long.
checkpoint.q4_fair_vs_market: Fair value is what the computer says based on stats, market price is what people actually paid. If they're different that's supposed to be the play — but honestly I'm just buying green candles and seeing what happens.

first_impression_elements: that massive orange 'real stats.' hit different — instantly grabbed
yellow CTA button is loud and chunky, can't miss it
dark grid background gives it that crypto terminal energy
ignored_elements: the 'How it works' secondary link — who reads that
the fine print dots below the CTA — skipped
data.visual_notes: Dark mode grid + orange accent is clean degen aesthetic, looks like something you'd actually ape into. Typography hierarchy is tight — headline does the heavy lifting and the color pop on 'real stats.' is the right move. No logo or nav but honestly for a squeeze page this hits, feels intentional not lazy.
